title: Ambient densities
summary: Defines ambient densities as homogeneous functions under positive dilation and restricts them to conformal densities on the hypersurface ρ = 0.

mathematicalContent.heading: Homogeneous ambient functions
mathematicalContent.body: An ambient density with character χ is a function F on the formal ambient bundle satisfying the dilation law F(δ_r U) = χ(r)F(U). For the usual real weight w, one takes χ(r) = r^w. The Lean definition allows a general commutative-monoid-valued character.

mathematicalContent.heading: Restriction to conformal data
mathematicalContent.body: Restricting F to the cone ρ = 0 gives a homogeneous function on the conformal metric ray bundle. Evaluating that boundary function along a chosen scale s produces its scale-dependent representative on M.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: AmbientWeight is not a new notion: it is the same multiplicative character of positive real dilations used for conformal densities.
In Ambient.Equivariant, a density of character χ is an α-valued function on the ray-model ambient bundle that transforms equivariantly under every positive dilation.
The boundary restriction pulls an ambient density back along the inclusion ι of the hypersurface ρ = 0. Its equivariance is inherited from the ambient dilation law.
correspondence.leanType: def restrictToBoundary (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : ConformalStructure.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: After restricting to ρ = 0, this definition evaluates the resulting conformal density on the section determined by each scale s.
correspondence.leanType: def restrictToScaleDependent (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : ConformalStructure.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: In Ambient.ConcreteEquivariant, the same equivariance condition is imposed on functions of actual pointwise metric representatives rather than conformal rays.

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, homogeneous functions on the metric bundle and ambient space
references.note: Source for interpreting conformal and ambient densities as homogeneous functions under dilation.

limitations: This module verifies the algebraic equivariance and restriction maps for arbitrary functions. It does not formalize smoothness, vector-bundle sections, the conventional identification χ(r)=r^w, or analytic extension and restriction results.
